errors.dashboardNotFoundDescription: This section does not exist or you may not have access to it.
errors.unexpectedErrorDescription: An unexpected error occurred while loading this page.
errors.criticalErrorDesc: A critical error occurred. Please try again or return to the dashboard.

dashboard.pageDescription: Configure providers, generate client config, and validate access from one place.
dashboard.publisherSubscriberDescription: Share your config template or subscribe to another user.
dashboard.integrationsDescription: Reference setup snippets for external clients.
dashboard.claudeCodeSentence: As an alternative, you can use CLIProxyAPI with Claude Code by setting environment variables before launching it.

password.sessionControlDescription: Immediately revoke all active user sessions across all devices.

telegram.syncTokensDescription: Generate tokens to sync OpenCode configurations
telegram.tokenOneTimeWarning: This token will only be shown once. Copy it now.
telegram.noTokensMessage: No sync tokens configured. Generate one to get started.
telegram.syncApiKeyNoKeysOption: No API keys — create one first
telegram.setupAutoInstallNote: The plugin will be auto-installed from npm when opencode starts.

settings.pageDescription: Manage account, security, config sync, and system operations.

confirmProxyUpdate.title: Update CLIProxyAPI
confirmProxyUpdate.message: Update CLIProxyAPI to {version}? The service will restart.

confirmDashboardUpdate.title: Update Dashboard
confirmDashboardUpdate.message: Update Dashboard to latest version? The container will restart.

confirmRevokeToken.title: Revoke Token
confirmRevokeToken.message: Are you sure you want to revoke this token?

confirmRevokeSessions.title: Force Logout All Users
confirmRevokeSessions.message: Force logout all users from all devices? This action cannot be undone.

settings.toastApiKeyUpdated: API key updated for sync token

apiKeys.pageDescription: Manage dashboard access keys for clients and integrations.
apiKeys.pageTooltip: API keys authenticate external tools (like the opencode-cliproxyapi-sync plugin) to access your dashboard configuration programmatically
apiKeys.emptyDescription: Create your first API key to access the dashboard programmatically
apiKeys.keyNameHint: Give your key a descriptive name for easy identification
apiKeys.keyShownOnce: This key will only be shown once. Store it securely.
apiKeys.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this API key?

providers.addKeyHint: Your API key will be stored securely and associated with your account
providers.removeConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to remove this key?
providers.toastAlreadyContributed: This API key has already been contributed
providers.validationProviderIdInvalid: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
providers.validationBaseUrlInvalid: Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL
providers.validationModelsRequired: At least one model mapping required
providers.toastFetchModelsInvalid: Please enter a valid Base URL (http/https) and API Key first
providers.pageDescription: Manage API keys, OAuth accounts, and custom provider endpoints in one place.
providers.keyLimitsDescription: Control how many provider keys each user can contribute
providers.maxKeysHint: Maximum number of provider keys a single user can contribute (current: {current})
providers.oauthPopupNote: OAuth flows open in a popup window. Make sure pop-ups are allowed in your browser.
providers.oauthIncognitoNote: Incognito mode is enabled. Browsers do not let the dashboard force-open a private window, so you will open the authorization URL manually.
providers.oauthUrlIncognitoTitle: Open This URL In A Private Window
providers.oauthOpenManually: If the popup did not appear, open this link manually.
providers.oauthOpenManuallyIncognito: Open this link manually in a new Firefox Private Window or browser incognito window, then continue the flow.
providers.oauthStep1Popup: Log in and authorize in the popup window.
providers.oauthStep1Incognito: Open the authorization URL above in a private/incognito window and sign in there.
providers.oauthStep2: After authorizing, the page will fail to load (this is expected).
providers.oauthStep3: Our server runs remotely, so the OAuth redirect can't reach it directly. Copy the FULL URL from the address bar.
providers.oauthStep4: Paste the URL below and submit.
providers.oauthDeviceStep1Popup: A browser window has opened with the authorization page.
providers.oauthDeviceStep1Incognito: Open the authorization URL above in a private/incognito window.
providers.oauthDeviceStep2: Log in and approve the access request.
providers.oauthDeviceStep3: Once approved, this dialog will update automatically.
providers.oauthPollingCallbackMsg: Callback submitted. Waiting for CLIProxyAPI to finish token exchange...
providers.oauthPollingNoCallbackMsg: Waiting for CLIProxyAPI to finish OAuth authorization...
providers.callbackMsgInvalidUrl: That doesn't look like a valid URL.
providers.callbackMsgMissingParams: URL must include both code and state parameters.
providers.callbackMsgValid: Callback URL looks good. Ready to submit.
providers.callbackMsgNoCallbackIncognito: No callback URL needed. Open the authorization URL below in a private/incognito window.
providers.callbackMsgNoCallbackPopup: No callback URL needed. Complete sign-in in the popup window.
providers.errorOAuthPopupBlocked: Popup blocked. Allow pop-ups and try again.
providers.errorOAuthMissingProviderState: Missing provider or state. Please restart the flow.
providers.errorOAuthCallbackNetwork: Network error while submitting callback URL.
providers.errorOAuthStartNetwork: Network error while starting OAuth flow.
providers.toastOAuthFollowStepsIncognito: Open the authorization URL below in a private/incognito window, then follow the steps.
providers.toastOAuthFollowStepsPopup: OAuth window opened. Follow the steps below.
providers.toastOAuthNoCallbackIncognito: Open the authorization URL below in a private/incognito window.
providers.toastOAuthNoCallbackPopup: OAuth window opened. Complete sign-in in the popup.
providers.noCustomProvidersDescription: Add an OpenAI-compatible provider to extend your AI capabilities
providers.deleteGroupMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this group? The providers inside will not be deleted, they will just become ungrouped. This action cannot be undone.
providers.perplexitySectionDescription: Browser session cookies for Perplexity Pro access
providers.perplexityStep1: Go to perplexity.ai and sign in to your Pro account
providers.perplexityStep3: Copy each cookie value and paste into the fields below
providers.perplexitySyncModelsDesc: Fetch latest models from sidecar and update proxy config
providers.toastPerplexitySavedProvisioned: Cookie saved & Perplexity Pro provider auto-configured

quota.pageDescription: View and manage API rate limits and quotas
quota.overallCapacityTooltip: Weighted average of remaining quota across all active provider accounts
quota.lowCapacityTooltip: Number of accounts with remaining quota below 20%

containers.emptyDescription: No Docker containers are currently running or available

config.toastProxyCleared: Proxy URL cleared. Retrying config load...
config.toastResetProxyFailed: Failed to reset proxy — the management API may be unreachable
config.toastNetworkErrorProxy: Network error — CLIProxyAPI may be completely unreachable through the proxy
config.warningText: Invalid configuration may prevent the service from starting. Review changes carefully before saving.
config.tipText: Changes are saved immediately to the management API. The service may need to be restarted for some configuration changes to take effect.
config.failedToLoadDescription: This can happen if an invalid proxy URL was configured, preventing CLIProxyAPI from responding.
config.proxyChangedMessage: Setting a proxy URL will route all CLIProxyAPI outbound traffic through "{url}". If the proxy is unreachable, you won't be able to load this configuration page anymore. Are you sure?
config.proxyValidationScheme: Proxy URL must start with socks5://, http://, or https:// (or use "direct"/"none" to bypass)
config.proxyValidationFormat: Invalid proxy URL format. Example: socks5://user:pass@host:port
config.failedToFetchYaml: Failed to fetch current config.yaml for YAML update

users.grantAdminDescription: Admins can manage users and access all system features
users.emptyState: No users found. Create one to get started.
users.toastPasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

logs.emptyState: No logs found. Logs will appear here when application events occur.
logs.clearAllLogsMessage: Are you sure you want to clear all logs?

setupWizard.title: Setup Wizard
setupWizard.successDescription: Your CLIProxyAPI instance is fully configured and ready to use.
setupWizard.step1Description: Open the Providers page in a new tab to add your first provider. This wizard will automatically detect when a provider is connected.
setupWizard.step2Locked: Complete Step 1 first to unlock this step.
setupWizard.step2ApiKeyWarning: Copy your API key now — it will not be shown again.
setupWizard.step2Hint: Give your key a memorable name, then click Create.
setupWizard.step3Locked: Complete the previous steps first to unlock this step.
setupWizard.step3WaitingModels: Waiting for models to become available...
setupWizard.stepDesc1: Add an OAuth account or configure an API key provider. Providers are the AI services that power your proxy (Claude, Gemini, Codex, and more).
setupWizard.stepDesc2: Generate a personal API key. This key is what your clients (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, etc.) use to authenticate with the proxy.
setupWizard.stepDesc3: Once a provider and API key are set up, the proxy exposes models automatically. This step confirms the catalog is populated and the proxy is reachable.
setupWizard.subtitle: Complete these steps to get CLIProxyAPI up and running.
setupWizard.autoRefreshNote: This page auto-refreshes every 5 seconds to detect when each step is complete.

updateNotification.dashboardDescription: A new version of the Dashboard is available. Would you like to update now?
updateNotification.proxyDescription: A new version of CLIProxyAPI (the proxy) is available. Would you like to update now?
updateNotification.updatingMessage: Updating to {version}... This may take a moment.

deploy.title: Dashboard Deployment
deploy.description: Deploy the latest dashboard changes from the repository
deploy.cacheDescription: Quick Update uses Docker cache for faster builds. Full Rebuild rebuilds everything from scratch.
deploy.webhookNotConfiguredDescription: The deployment webhook is not set up. To enable dashboard deployments from the UI, you need to configure the webhook server on your host machine.
deploy.confirmMessage: Start {mode}? The dashboard will restart after deployment.

configSharing.publishDescription: Share your CLIProxyAPI configuration with others. Generate a unique share code that others can use to automatically sync with your config settings.
configSharing.shareCodeHint: Others can use this code to subscribe to your config updates
configSharing.confirmUnpublishMessage: Are you sure you want to unpublish? All subscribers will lose access.
configSharing.subscribeDescription: Subscribe to someone else's CLIProxyAPI configuration. Your model selection will be automatically controlled by the publisher until you unsubscribe.
configSharing.noApiKeyWarning: You need at least one API key to subscribe. Please add an API key first.
configSharing.subscribedControlledMessage: While subscribed, model selection is controlled by {publisher}. Your previous preferences will be restored when you unsubscribe.
configSharing.confirmUnsubscribeMessage: Are you sure you want to unsubscribe? Your previous config settings will be restored.
configSharing.toastNoApiKey: Cannot subscribe without an API key. Please add one first.

quickStartConfig.registersAgentsPrefix: Registers agents and hooks in OpenCode. Use
quickStartConfig.registersAgentsSuffix: to also install recommended skills (simplify, cartography).

modelSelector.title: Model Selection
modelSelector.tooltip: Deselect models to exclude them from your config. Excluded models won't appear in opencode.json or be assigned to agents.
modelSelector.lockedMessage: Model selection is controlled by your publisher. Unsubscribe to regain control.

quotaAlerts.sectionDescription: Get notified when quota drops below a threshold.
quotaAlerts.botTokenHint: Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram
quotaAlerts.chatIdHint: Your Telegram user/group ID. Use @userinfobot to find it
quotaAlerts.thresholdHint: Alert when any account drops below this capacity
quotaAlerts.checkIntervalHint: How often to check quota levels (1-1440 min, default: 5)
quotaAlerts.cooldownHint: Minimum time between notifications (1-1440 min, default: 60)
quotaAlerts.monitoredProvidersHint: Only selected providers will trigger alerts
quotaAlerts.toastTestSent: Test message sent! Check your Telegram.

agentConfig.tlsNotice: TLS is typically handled by Caddy reverse proxy. Only configure this for direct TLS termination.
agentConfig.pprofNotice: Go runtime profiling. Only enable for debugging.
agentConfig.claudeHeaderDesc: Custom headers sent with all Claude API requests
agentConfig.ampCodeDesc: Configuration for Amp Code upstream integration
agentConfig.oauthAliasesDesc: Override model names for OAuth providers. Each provider has a list of model name mappings.
agentConfig.payloadDesc: Override or filter request payloads sent to upstream providers. Values are JSON.
agentConfig.fieldProxyUrlDesc: Optional SOCKS5/HTTP/HTTPS proxy for outbound requests. Use 'direct' or 'none' to bypass. Leave empty for direct connection.
agentConfig.fieldAuthDirDesc: Directory where OAuth credential files are stored.
agentConfig.fieldForceModelPrefixDesc: Require model names to include a provider prefix
agentConfig.fieldPassthroughHeadersDesc: Forward client headers to upstream providers
agentConfig.fieldIncognitoBrowserDesc: Use incognito mode for browser-based OAuth flows
agentConfig.fieldNonstreamKeepaliveDesc: Emit blank lines every N seconds for non-streaming responses to prevent idle timeouts (0 = disabled)
agentConfig.fieldRequestRetryDesc: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed requests
agentConfig.fieldRoutingStrategyDesc: Load balancing strategy for multiple providers
agentConfig.fieldSwitchProjectDesc: Automatically switch to another project when quota is exceeded
agentConfig.fieldSwitchPreviewModelLabel: Switch Preview Model on Quota Exceeded
agentConfig.fieldSwitchPreviewModelDesc: Fall back to preview models when quota is exceeded
agentConfig.fieldLogsMaxSizeDesc: Maximum total size of all log files (0 = unlimited)
agentConfig.fieldErrorLogsMaxFilesDesc: Maximum number of error log files to retain
agentConfig.fieldTlsKeyDesc: Path to TLS private key file
agentConfig.fieldAmpRestrictLocalhostDesc: Restrict management API to localhost only
agentConfig.payloadFieldDefault: Default payload fields merged into every request
agentConfig.payloadFieldOverride: Payload fields that override request values

openCodeConfig.noProvidersDesc: You need to configure at least one AI provider before generating an OpenCode config. Head to the
openCodeConfig.noProvidersDescMid: page to add Gemini, Claude, Codex, or OpenAI Compatible keys, or set up
openCodeConfig.apiKeyRequiredDesc: Create an API key to generate your configuration.
openCodeConfig.noModelsDesc: Providers are configured, but no models were discovered yet. If you just added providers, wait a moment and refresh. If the issue persists, verify provider credentials on the
openCodeConfig.oauthConnected: OAuth providers connected — using placeholder key
openCodeConfig.noApiKeysDesc: Create an API key on the
openCodeConfig.noApiKeysDescMid: page or connect an OAuth provider on the
openCodeConfig.noApiKeysDescSuffix: page. The config below uses a placeholder.
openCodeConfig.defaultModelTooltip: Choose one of the available OpenCode models for the `model` field in opencode.json. If you already saved a custom value that is not in the discovered list, it will appear as a custom option.
openCodeConfig.defaultModelHint: Choose a discovered model, or leave it on auto fallback to use
openCodeConfig.variantTooltip: Normal: 9 specialized agents with categories for fine-grained control. Slim: 6 agents, lower token usage, built-in fallback chains. Both use your proxy models.

ohMyOpenCode.apiKeyRequiredDesc: Create an API key to generate your configuration.
ohMyOpenCode.noProvidersDesc: You need to configure at least one AI provider before generating an Oh My Open Agent config. Head to the
ohMyOpenCode.noProvidersDescSuffix: page to add Gemini, Claude, Codex, or OpenAI Compatible keys, or set up OAuth providers.
ohMyOpenCode.assignmentsDesc: Assignments are grouped by tier so core agents stay separated from fast and creative workflows. Click any model to override it. Changes save automatically and sync via Config Sync.

ohMyOpenCodeSlim.apiKeyRequiredDesc: Create an API key to generate your slim configuration.
ohMyOpenCodeSlim.noProvidersDesc: Configure at least one AI provider before generating a slim config. Head to
ohMyOpenCodeSlim.description: Slim uses 6 specialized agents (orchestrator, oracle, designer, explorer, librarian, fixer) with a dedicated fallback system. Changes save automatically.
ohMyOpenCodeSlim.toastSaveFailed: Failed to save slim config — reverted
